---
title: "Creel Dashboard"
output:
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    orientation: rows
    vertical_layout: fill
    highlight: null
runtime: shiny_prerendered
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE
)

library(tidycreel)
library(ggplot2)

data(example_calendar)
data(example_counts)
data(example_interviews)
data(example_lengths)

design <- creel_design(example_calendar, date = date, strata = day_type)
design <- add_counts(design, example_counts)
design <- add_interviews(
  design,
  example_interviews,
  catch = catch_total,
  effort = hours_fished,
  harvest = catch_kept,
  trip_status = trip_status,
  trip_duration = trip_duration,
  species_sought = species_sought,
  n_anglers = n_anglers
)
design <- add_lengths(
  design,
  example_lengths,
  length_uid = interview_id,
  interview_uid = interview_id,
  species = species,
  length = length,
  length_type = length_type,
  count = count,
  release_format = "binned"
)

effort_total <- estimate_effort(design)
effort_by_day <- estimate_effort(design, by = day_type)
length_dist <- est_length_distribution(design, by = species, bin_width = 25)
report_tbl <- data.frame(
  Metric = c(
    "Total effort",
    "SE",
    "Lower CI",
    "Upper CI"
  ),
  Value = c(
    round(effort_total$estimates$estimate, 1),
    round(effort_total$estimates$se, 1),
    round(effort_total$estimates$ci_lower, 1),
    round(effort_total$estimates$ci_upper, 1)
  )
)
```

Row
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Overview

The template starts from tidycreel's bundled example data and shows one simple
pattern for turning a `creel_design` into a dashboard-style report.

```{r overview-table}
knitr::kable(
  data.frame(
    Metric = c(
      "Survey days",
      "Interview records",
      "Estimated total effort",
      "Length-distribution groups"
    ),
    Value = c(
      nrow(example_calendar),
      nrow(example_interviews),
      round(effort_total$estimates$estimate, 1),
      length(unique(length_dist$species))
    )
  ),
  col.names = c("Metric", "Value")
)
```

### Total Effort

```{r effort-plot}
ggplot2::autoplot(effort_total, title = "Estimated total effort", theme = "creel")
```

Row
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Effort by Day Type

```{r effort-day-type}
ggplot2::autoplot(effort_by_day, title = "Effort by day type", theme = "creel")
```

### Weighted Length Distribution

```{r length-plot}
ggplot2::autoplot(
  length_dist,
  title = "Weighted length distribution by species",
  theme = "creel"
)
```

Row
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Report Table

```{r report-table}
knitr::kable(report_tbl)
```

### Notes

- Replace the example datasets with your own design, interview, and length data.
- Use `theme = "creel"` in `autoplot()` calls for package-standard styling.
- Extend this skeleton with additional estimates, tables, and narrative sections
  as your reporting needs grow.
